scope: FQ-13P1: consume the enemy variant pools the FQ-13P0 audit surfaced as AVAILABLE_NOT_CONSUMED - deterministic, lifetime-stable per-enemy sprite selection with a clean fallback chain. No gameplay math, save-schema, or new-art change.
summary: The FQ-13P0 audit's headline finding was that nine valid enemy variant PNGs (cave_crawler/raider_basic/surface_slime _01..03) sat on disk while simple_threat drew only the canonical single. FQ-13P1 wires them the same way blocks already work. A new simple_threat._select_sprite, run once in _ready, prefers a deterministic variant from visual_variant_textures('enemies', enemy_id), falling back to the canonical visual_texture and then to the existing code-drawn body; whichever it picks is stored in _art and held for the enemy's entire life, so the sprite never changes under damage, movement, pause, or redraw. The selector variant_for(id, cell, seed, pool_size) = posmod(hash('id:cell:seed'), pool_size) mirrors the block variant rule and salts the hash with enemy_id so two different kinds spawning at one cell can still differ; it returns -1 for an empty pool, recorded in a new variant_index field. Crucially the choice is presentation-only and adds nothing to saves: serialize_threats already round-trips position and enemy_id, so a reloaded enemy recomputes the exact same variant from the same cell and world seed. The audit tool now lists 'enemies' among VARIANT_CONSUMERS and reports zero findings and zero data bugs, and docs/UI_ASSET_GAPS.md marks enemy variants LIVE and records the operator-confirmed full-body-pool decision for future player cosmetic variation. Five smoke checks prove pool discovery, that more than one variant is selected across forty deterministic inputs, that identical inputs are repeatable, that a spawned crawler keeps variant 2 and its texture through a hit plus redraw plus physics frames, and that a thornrat (no pool, no canonical art) correctly falls back to the code-drawn body. Suite 283 -> 288. The Node2D-typed-world inference trap that hung the two previous increments was avoided by explicitly typing the world.cell_of/world.world_seed reads.
acceptance: Valid enemy variant pool discovered at runtime (fq13p1_enemy_pool_discovered: cave_crawler pool=3)
More than one variant selected across different deterministic inputs (fq13p1_variants_differ: 3 distinct over 40 cells)
Same inputs always yield the same choice (fq13p1_selection_deterministic)
One variant chosen at creation and stable through damage, redraw, and physics frames, index in-pool (fq13p1_selection_stable: idx 2->2)
Missing pool + missing canonical falls back to the code-drawn body (fq13p1_fallback_code_drawn: thornrat _art null, variant_index -1; crawler has art)
Presentation-only: variant recomputed from position+enemy_id on load, nothing added to saves
Suite 288/288; validator and capsule doctor green; asset_audit --strict 0 findings/0 data bugs
validation: python scripts/asset_audit.py --strict -> exit 0 (0 findings, 0 data bugs)
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited headless run -> 288/288 PASS, 5 new fq13p1_* checks green
git diff --check -> 0 whitespace errors (LF->CRLF notices only)
decisions: Mirror the block variant rule for enemies (posmod(hash) selection) rather than inventing a new scheme; salt with enemy_id so different kinds at one cell can differ
Choose once in _ready and store in _art/variant_index; _draw is unchanged so there is no per-frame reselection
Do not save the variant: position + enemy_id already round-trip, so the identical choice is recomputed on load - keeps saves lean and matches the block no-save rule
Fallback chain pool -> canonical -> code-drawn, so the FQ-13 enemies with no art degrade cleanly
Explicitly type the world.cell_of/world.world_seed reads to avoid the Node2D-typed-world := inference trap that hung the two previous increments
Self-review only (no agent spawned) for a presentation-only change covered by the validator + 5 smoke checks
risks: Only the three original enemies have variant pools; the FQ-13 enemies (thornrat/ore_tick/torchbearer) have no art and fall back to the code-drawn body - authored variants are an art-backlog task
Variant salting uses the spawn cell, so two enemies in the exact same cell with the same id pick the same variant (acceptable; keeps save/load determinism trivial)
next_action: FQ-13P2-P4: generated UI/orb/slot placeholders, player full-body cosmetic pool, opening variant-vs-animation distinction.
